name: Canning Pumpkins & Winter Squash (Cubed)
recipeIngredient: An average of 16 pounds is needed for a canner load of 7 quarts
An average of 10 pounds is needed for a canner load of 9 pints
An average of 2-1/4 pounds per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose pumpkins and squash with a hard rind and stringless, mature pulp for best cooking quality.
2. Wash the fruit, remove seeds, cut into 1-inch-wide slices, and peel.
3. Cut the flesh into 1-inch cubes.
4. Boil the cubes in water for 2 minutes only; do not mash or puree.
5. Fill jars with cubes and enough cooking liquid to leave a 1-inch headspace.
6. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 and Table 2 recommendations.
7. For pies, drain the jars and strain or sieve the cubes.
